(1) These rules may be called the Environment (Protection) Amendment Rules, 2004. (2) They shall come into force on the date of their publication in the Official Gazette. 2. In the Environment (Protection) Rules, 1986, in Schedule 1, in serial number 95 relating to EMISSION LIMITS FOR NEW DIESEL ENGINES (UP TO 800 KW) FOR GENERATOR SETS (GENSETS) APPLICATIONS, in paragraph 1,- ¢ (i) in Note I, Note Ii and Note lll, for the word “manufacturers” or ‘manufacturer”, wherever they occur, the word ‘supplier’ shall be substituted ; -- 2 of 3 -- [um I—awE 33)1 WIRE 1 T ¢ STEHR (i) after Note III, the following Exptanation shall be inserted, namely :- “Explanation - For the purposes of this paragraph, ‘supplier means manufacturer of new diesel engines for genset applications in India and importer of such diesel engines for genset applications and diesel gensets imported into India”. [F.No. Q-15022/2/2001-CPA] C. VISWANATH, Jt. Secy. Note:— The princigal rules were published in the Gazette of India vide number S.0. 844(E) dated 19™ November, 1986 and subsequently amended vide S.0. 433(E) dated 18" April, 1987, S.0.
